goal: Establish failing Phase 0 ground-truth tests for current dependency graph integrity, identity, import resolution, call resolution, and graph validation failures before any refactor implementation.
plan_type_rationale: This is a single gated phase in a larger ordered refactor. The work must stop after failing tests exist.

hard_stops.reason: Stop after failing tests are confirmed; do not implement Phase 1 scanner or graph fixes in this workorder.

phases.name: Phase 0 - Establish Ground Truth
phases.description: Add or confirm failing tests that expose current graph integrity, identity, relationship, and validation failures without changing production code.

tasks.description: Read current test patterns, graph pipeline code, and existing graph artifacts needed to design targeted Phase 0 characterization tests.

Add or update tests that expose graph edges whose endpoints are not node IDs, duplicate identities, unresolved imports, unresolved calls, alias imports, duplicate function names, nested functions, and class/object method calls.
Add a graph validation test requiring every resolved edge endpoint to exist in nodes, every unresolved edge to include resolutionStatus, and every ambiguous edge to include candidate IDs.
Run the targeted tests and confirm they fail against the current implementation for the expected graph integrity and resolution reasons.
Document the exact failing assertions in the workorder execution notes so Phase 1 cannot proceed without visible ground truth.
Stop execution after failing tests exist. Do not modify scanner, relationship extraction, import resolution, call resolution, graph construction, indexing, or documentation behavior.
